attributes.title: Sigma Chi Province Workshop
attributes.description: Sigma Chi – California Central Valley Province Workshop. We’re looking forward to bringing brothers together for a focused and productive day of leadership development, collaboration, and best-practice sharing across the province. Brothers are invited from across the Central Valley province, and will be visiting for the day.The event will begin with all the invited chapters, coming together to go through a small workshop, and then going into breakout room intermittently. One large room is going to be reserved, as well as 7 breakout rooms.
attributes.location_at: COB 105, COB 110, COB 114, COB 113, COB 263, COB 267, COB 265

attributes.title: Pulso
attributes.description: The Hermanas Unidas (HaU) Pulso is an annual event dedicated to honoring and celebrating the hermandad—our sisterhood—that defines our organization. This event serves as a meaningful gathering for our current chapter members to reflect on our shared values, recognize our collective achievements throughout the academic year, and strengthen the bonds that unite us as Hermanas.The primary purpose of this event is to foster unity, empowerment, and cultural pride among our members while also extending that sense of community to the broader campus. As an organization rooted in supporting Latinas and promoting cultural awareness, this celebration highlights the importance of embracing and preserving our cultural heritage. Through music, dance, and community engagement, we create a space where traditions are honored and shared.A central feature of the event is a live band performance, which enhances the cultural experience by showcasing music that reflects our heritage and traditions. Dance serves not only as entertainment but as a cultural expression that connects generations and communities. By incorporating live music, we aim to create an authentic and immersive atmosphere that encourages participation and appreciation.This event is open to the entire campus community. Inclusivity is a core value of Hermanas Unidas, and we intentionally design this celebration to welcome students, faculty, and staff from all backgrounds. By opening our event to the public, we provide an opportunity for others to learn about, experience, and celebrate the richness of our culture alongside us. It promotes cross-cultural understanding, diversity appreciation, and campus-wide unity.Overall, the Hermanas Unidas Pulso is more than a social gathering; it is a purposeful event that reinforces sisterhood, celebrates cultural identity, and strengthens community connections within and beyond our organization.
attributes.location_at: Dr. Vikram and Priya Lakireddy Grand Ballroom

attributes.title: Prayer Night
attributes.description: Join us for Chi Alpha Prayer at UC Merced! Fridays at 7 pm in GLACIER 160, we gather to seek God together through prayer, worship, and encouragement. It's a peaceful space to recharge, connect with God, and support one another. All are welcome, whether you're new to prayer or have been walking with Jesus for years.

attributes.title: Anchor Spike
attributes.description: Anchor Spike is a one-day open event volleyball tournament where we will be fundraising for service for sight while bringing together students and community members for a competitive and engaging event that supports delta gammas philanthropy. All proceeds from the event will be donated to the Delta Gamma Foundation and the Center for Vision Enhancement located here in Merced to support individuals who are blind or visually impaired.

attributes.title: Un Sueño en Mexico
attributes.description: Ballet Folklorico de UCM is hosting their anual end of the year showcase. This event showcases the beauty of Mexican culture through the tradition of folk dances that hold significance and meaning to our culture. This is a three-hour long event of dancing and music where our club members will be on stage dancing to over 20 songs that they have learned throughout the fall and spring semester. This showcase not only demonstrates the cultural traditions of Mexican folk dance, but it also shows the dedication and the hard work of our club members. It's an opportunity for our members to grow their performance skills by allowing them to dance on a stage in a theater that will allow them to show all they have learned in regards to keeping the Mexican tradition alive through dance.
attributes.location_at: Merced Theater 301 W Main Street Merced CA, 935340

attributes.description: Join ASBMB for Earth Day at UC Merced, a campus event focused on environmental awareness and sustainability. Stop by to learn how you can get involved, support sustainable practices, and connect with fellow students who are passionate about making a positive impact!

attributes.title: Agricultural Insects and Plants Monthly Meeting
attributes.description: Agricultural Insects and Plants Monthly Meeting is a regular meeting where graduate and undergraduate students discuss insects and plants that are important in agriculture. Participants talk about insects that damage crops, beneficial insects such as pollinators and natural enemies, and how plants respond to insect feeding. They share research findings, field observations, and practical, environmentally responsible pest management approaches. The group also discusses potential future events, outreach activities, career paths, and related academic or professional opportunities. The overall purpose is to exchange knowledge and improve how agricultural systems are understood and managed.

attributes.title: Spring Break Trip
attributes.description: College Life (Young Life) Spring Retreat: Six Flags AdventureJoin College Life for a weekend of high-adrenaline team bonding! This retreat is designed to strengthen our community through shared experiences and intentional connection.Itinerary & Activities:Friday, March 27th: We will depart via carpool from various CA locations to Santa Clarita. Once we check into our local hotel (location finalized post-registration), we’ll kick off the weekend with team-building games and a movie night to foster a sense of belonging among members.Saturday, March 28th: A full day of bonding at Six Flags Magic Mountain, building resilience and friendship through a day of adventure.Sunday, March 29th: We will conclude the retreat with a group brunch and a topical Bible Study, providing a space for students to reflect and connect spiritually before heading back.Travel & Safety: To ensure this trip is accessible, we are utilizing a carpool system.
